observations.recommendation: In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the LCE, faithfully: a. Mandate the Inventory Committee to conduct an annual detailed physical count of all PPEs as of a specific date and submit the RPCPPE to the audit team not later than January 31, of each year.
b. Require the General Services Officer to maintain and update his property cards per category of PPE, pursuant to Section 119 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I;
c. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update his property ledger cards per category of PPE, pursuant to Section 120 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I; and
d. Ensure that the General Services Office and Municipal Accountant reconcile their property cards and property ledger cards, respectively.
d. Prepare and submit the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) for Calendar Year 2021, in compliance with Section 121 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We highly recommended that the LCE: a. Direct the Accountant to record all regular procurements of supplies in their respective inventory accounts, record the same as expense when issuance thereof takes place, and maintain the appropriate ledgers;
c. Direct the two offices to religiously reconcile records with the ledgers on a regular basis.

All funds received as trust for LGU’s implementation shall be recorded as TRUST LIABILITY and all expenses, including bank charges, related thereto are capitalized as part of the cost of the project under trust. Necessary adjustments for the effect of said transactions should be done.

1.7 We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant
to reconcile the reciprocal accounts, Due from Other Funds and Due
to Other Funds Accounts and effect adjustments to reflect the correct
account balances and present reliable data pursuant to Section 111(2)
of Presidential Decree No. 1445.

2.7 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) instruct the
Municipal Accountant to prepare the BRS on a monthly basis in
accordance with Section 74 of the PD No. 1445 and Sections 3.2 and
3.4 of the COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 and submit
regularly to the auditor for review and verification.

3.10 We reiterated our prior years’ audit recommendations that the LCE,
faithfully:
a. Mandate the Inventory Committee to conduct an annual
detailed physical count of all PPEs as of a specific date and
submit the RPCPPE to the audit team not later than January
31, of each year.
b. Require the General Services Officer to maintain and update
his property cards per category of PPE, pursuant to Section
119 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I;
c. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update his
property ledger cards per category of PPE, pursuant to Section
120 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I; and
d. Ensure that the General Services Office and Municipal
Accountant reconcile their property cards and property ledger
cards, respectively.

4.5 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the
necessary adjusting entries to record the appropriate accounts. It is
further recommended that future transactions be analyzed and
classified as to the prescribed accounts in Section 1(2) of the COA
Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015 in order to avoid
misstatements and to fairly present the balances in the financial
statements.

5.9 We highly recommended that the LCE to:
a) Direct the Municipal Accountant to record all regular
procurements of supplies in their respective inventory accounts,
record the same as expense when issuance thereof takes
place, and maintain the appropriate ledgers;
b) Direct the General Services Officer to religiously maintain the
necessary inventory and supplies’ stock cards; and
c) Direct the two offices to religiously reconcile records with the
ledgers on a regular basis.

6.6 We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Appropriately disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the
details of the Loans Payable pursuant to Section 127 IPSAS No. 1;
and
b. Reclassify loan amortizations due for the next twelve months to
current liabilities, and non-current liabilities for loan which is due
more than twelve months pursuant to Section 80 of the IPSAS No.
1.

7.7 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Reclassify the PPE accounts below ₱50,000.00 threshold to its
appropriate semi-expendable properties and expenses; and
b. Adjust the opening balance in the Statement of Changes in Net
Assets/Equity in compliance to the COA Circular No. 2022-004,
dated May 31, 2022.

8.5 We recommended that the General Services Officer facilitate the
issuance of the accompanying ICS of the semi-expendable properties,
pursuant to Section 4.1 of the COA Circular No. 2022-004, dated May
31, 2022.

Therefore, we highly recommended that the LCE: a. Instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct immediately an extensive inventory of the road network system under the Municipality’s jurisdiction and prepare a report thereon;
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to religiously update the subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network based on the result of the physical count
c. Further require the Municipal Accountant to prepare a lapsing schedule for computation of the depreciation at the end of each year
d. Instruct the GSO to religiously maintain Local Road Inventory and Road Map
e. Likewise, have the GSO keep an updated complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components; and
f. Finally, require the Municipal Engineer to provide the Accountant and the General Services Office with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.

We recommended the LCE to instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the BRS on a monthly basis, in accordance with Section 74 of P.D. No. 1445, otherwise known as The Government Auditing Code of the Philippines and Sections 3.2 and 3.4 of the COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.
We recommended the Management to: a. Instruct the GSO to keep and maintain Stock Cards for each and every supply to have an adequate monitoring and immediate information of supplies on hand
b. Require the Accounting Division to keep and maintain Supplies Ledger Cards to record supplies received, issued and the balance both in quantity and amount;
c. Ensure proper coordination between GSO and Accounting Division to have an updated and reconciled balances in their Stock Cards and Supplies Ledger Cards, respectively; and

We recommended the Municipality to submit the RPCI for the first and second semesters, not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year, respectively, in compliance to Section 124 of Volume I of the MNGAS, for LGUs.

We recommended that the BAC prepares the PMR with the approval by the Head of the Procuring Entity and submit the same to the GPPB printed and electronic format within 14 calendar days after the end of each semester and the Annual Procurement Plan to GPPB on or before end of January of the budget year.
We recommended to the GSO to facilitate the issuance of the accompanying PAR of the properties, pursuant to Section 123, Volume I of the MNGAS for LGUs.
b. Direct the General Services Officer to religiously maintain the necessary inventory and supplies’ stock cards

We recommend that the Management require the creation of Inventory Committee to conduct actual physical inventory and the count of Property, Plant and Equipment annually taking into consideration the required standards and procedures. Further, we recommend the management to submit Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) for CY 2020 to this Commission in compliance to the provisions of Section 156 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992, Paragraph 2 of Section 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units, Volume I, and Section 105 of the Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise known as The Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.
observations.reason: Unreconciled records of the Accountant General Services Officer.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Accountant to appropriately disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the details of the Loans Payable, pursuant to paragraph 127(a)(b)(c) of International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) No. 1 and Require the Municipal Accountant to reclassify loan amortizations due for the next twelve months to current liabilities and non-current liabilities for loan which is due more than twelve months, pursuant to Section 80 of the International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) 1 – Presentation of Financial Statements.

1.10 In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Local Chief
Executive (LCE), strictly:
a) Inventory Committee shall prepare the Report on the Physical
Count of Property, Plant and Equipment, in compliance with
Section 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting
System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume
I;
b) General Services Officer shall maintain and update his property
cards per category of property, plant and equipment, pursuant
to Section 119 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I;
c) Municipal Accountant shall maintain and update his property
ledger cards per category of property, plant and equipment,
pursuant to Section 120 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I;
d) Ensure that the General Services Office and Municipal
Accountant shall reconcile their property cards and property
ledger cards, respectively; and
e) Mandate the Inventory Committee to conduct an annual detailed
physical count of all PPEs as of a specific date and submit the
RPCPPE to the audit team not later than January 31, of each
year.

2.6 Therefore, we highly recommend that the Local Chief Executive
(LCE):
a) Instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct immediately
an extensive inventory of the road network system under the
Municipality’s jurisdiction and prepare a report thereon;
b) Require the Municipal Accountant to religiously update the
subsidiary records for roads and road components for every
road network based on the result of the physical count;
c) Further require the Municipal Accountant to prepare a lapsing
schedule for computation of the depreciation at the end of each
year;
d) Instruct the General Services Officer (GSO) to religiously
maintain Local Road Inventory and Road Map;
e) Likewise, have the GSO keep an updated complete Local Road
Network Property Card for all roads and its components; and
f) Finally, require the Municipal Engineer to provide the
Accountant and the General Services Office with the complete
description and cost segregation of road components for road
projects.

3.7 Therefore, we highly reiterated our previous year’s recommendation
that the Local Chief Executive (LCE):
Shall instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Bank
Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis, in accordance
with Section 74 of Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise
known as The Government Auditing Code of the Philippines and
Sections 3.2 and 3.4 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No.
96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

4.12 We recommend the Management to:
a. Instruct the GSO to keep and maintain Stock Cards for each and
every supply to have an adequate monitoring and immediate
information of supplies on hand;
b. Require the Accounting Division to keep and maintain Supplies
Ledger Cards to record supplies received, issued and the balance
both in quantity and amount;
c. Ensure proper coordination between GSO and Accounting
Division to have an updated and reconciled balances in their Stock
Cards and Supplies Ledger Cards, respectively; and
d. Prepare and submit the Summary of Supplies and Materials
Issued (SSMI) for Calendar Year 2021, in compliance with Section
121 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for
Local Government Units, Volume I.

5.5 We recommend the Municipality to submit the Report on the Physical
Count of Inventories (RPCI) for the first and second semesters, not
later than July 31 and January 31 of each year, respectively, in
compliance to Section 124 of Volume I of the Manual on New
Government Accounting System (MNGAS), for Local Government
Units (LGUs).

6.7 We recommend that the Bids and Award Committee prepares the
Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) with the approval by the Head of the Procuring Entity and submit the same to the GPPB in
printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days after
the end of each semester and the Annual Procurement Plan to GPPB
on or before end of January of the budget year.

7.4 We recommend to the General Services Officer (GSO) to facilitate the
issuance of the accompanying Acknowledgement Receipt for
Equipment (ARE) of the properties, pursuant to Section 123, Volume I of
the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS), for
Local Government Units (LGUs).

8.9 Due to the foregoing lapses noted, we highly recommended that the
Local Chief Executive:
a) Direct the Accountant to record all regular procurements of
supplies in their respective inventory accounts, record the same
as expense when issuance thereof takes place, and maintain the
appropriate ledgers;
b) Direct the General Services Officer to religiously maintain the
necessary inventory and supplies’ stock cards; and
c) Direct the two offices to religiously reconcile records with the
ledgers on a regular basis;

The Head of Agency shall instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Bank Reconciliation for CY 2020. Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis in accordance with Section 74 of Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise known as The Government Auditing Code of the Philippines and Sections 3.2 and 3.4 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

We recommend Management to strictly comply with the limitations for the appropriation for discretionary funds provided in Section 325 (h) of R.A. 7160 and refer to Section 19 of the General Appropriations Act of 1989 as guide for its utilization. Furthermore, direct the municipal accountant to adjust the accounts inappropriately charged and refer to the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for Use in Local Government Units as prescribed by COA Circular 2002-003 dated June 20, 2002 as guide for future purposes.

Designate an employee who will be assigned to prepare, monitor and submit the required Monthly Fuel Consumption Report; and Ensure that the forms, records, and reports prescribed under COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1997 on the reporting and control over fuel consumptions are prepared, submitted and properly accomplished so that wasteful, excessive and unnecessary expenditures are minimized and disbursements are effectively controlled and properly accounted for.

We recommend the management to prepare the Annual Procurement Plan (APP) and the Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) to adhere to the rules and regulations of Republic Act No. 9184 and its Implementing Rules and Regulation (IRR).

All funds received as trust implementation shall be recorded as TRUST LIABILITY and all expenses, including bank charges, related thereto even are capitalized as part of the cost of the project under trust. observations.recommendation:
The Head of Agency shall instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Bank
Reconciliation Statement (BRS) on a monthly basis in accordance with Section 74
of Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise known as The Government
Auditing Code of the Philippines and Sections 3.2 and 3.4 of the Commission on
Audit (COA) Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

We recommend that the Management require the creation of Inventory
Committee to conduct actual physical inventory count of Property, Plant and
Equipment annually taking into consideration the required standards and
procedures. Further, we recommend the management to submit Report on the
Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) for CY 2020 to this
Commission in compliance to the provisions of Section 156 of the Commission on
Audit (COA) Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992, Paragraph 2 of Section
124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local
Government Units, Volume I, and Section 105 of the Presidential Decree (P.D.) No.
1445, otherwise known as The Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.

We recommend Management to strictly comply with the limitations for the
appropriation for discretionary funds provided in Section 325 (h) of R.A. 7160 and
refer to Section 19 of the General Appropriations Act of 1989 as guide for its
utilization. Furthermore, direct the municipal accountant to adjust the accounts
inappropriately charged and refer to the Manual on the New Government
Accounting System (NGAS) for Use in Local Government Units as prescribed by
COA Circular 2002-003 dated June 20, 2002 as guide for future purposes.

observations.reason: This LGU is the epicenter of the political disputes between or among politicians and even reached the national attention. Upon inquiry, Mr Danny Dimapalao was at-large or absconded sometimes in 1994. Per information, he died during the time of Late Governor Datu Andal Ampatuan, Sr. The latter was then the municipal mayor whom Mr. Dimapalao was the treasurer at the time. The situation was so volatile just because of the political condition of place. Martial was declared

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management require the Inventory
Committee/General Services Office (GSO) to conduct actual physical inventory
count of Property, Plant and Equipment annually taking into consideration the
required standards and procedures. Further, we recommend the management to
submit Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment
(RPCPPE) CY 2019 to this Commission, in accordance with Section 156 of the
Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992, and
Section 114 and Paragraph 2 of Section 124 of the Manual on the New
Government Accounting System (MNGAS), for Local Government Units,
Volume 1.

1. Require the Municipal Accountant to appropriately disclose in the
Notes to Financial Statements the details of the Loans Payable,
pursuant to paragraph 127(a)(b)(c) of International Public Sector
Accounting Standards (IPSAS) No. 1.
2. Require the Municipal Accountant to reclassify loan amortizations due for
the next twelve months to current liabilities and non-current liabilities for
loan which is due more than twelve months, pursuant to Section 80 of the
International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) 1 – Presentation
of Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Budget Officer should prepare and submit the SCBAA as of December 31, 2019
as a component of the Financial Statements and to show whether the LGU was
compliant with its approved budget, pursuant to Paragraph 21 (e) of the
Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standard (PPSAS) 1 – Presentation of
Financial Statements and Paragraph 1 of the PPSAS 24 – Presentation of Budget
Information in Financial Statements.

We recommend the management the following:
a. Require the Municipal Treasurer to adhere strictly to the provision that all
payment should be made by check directly payable to the suppliers in
accordance with Section 40 of the Manual on the New Government
Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs) and
deduct taxes due for remittance to BIR, pursuant to Section 2(a) of the
Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR) Revenue Regulations No. 1-87 dated April
2, 1987;
b. Stop granting reimbursement to all Municipal officials and employees;
c. Ensure that the proper procurement process is implemented by the
responsible procurement Department Head.

Adjustment to effect the errors is hereby recommended by the municipal accountant in order reflect the correct value of the assets affected. Any adjustment that might be resulted therefrom shall be reflected on the financial statements in the subsequent year.

All funds received as trust for LGU’s implementation shall be recorded as TRUST LIABILITY and all expenses, including bank charges, related thereto are capitalized as part of the cost of the project under trust. Necessary adjustments for the effect of said transactions should be done.

1.) Prepare and implement a Solid Waste Management Plan as provided under RA 9003 and submit the same to the auditor concerned; 2.) Formulate a detailed procedures and guidelines to implement SWM System; 3.) Information and education campaign to reduce if not prevent illegal disposal of the garbage.

Adjustment to effect the errors is hereby recommended by the municipal
accountant in order reflect the correct value of the assets affected. Any adjustment
that might be resulted therefrom shall be reflected on the financial statements in
the subsequent year.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant should follow the standards as set
forth on PPSAS even assuming that the total effect of the errors has no material
effect in the over-all presentation of the financial statements taken as whole.

A reconciliation of the records between the LGU and DBP must be made to look into the difference of the amount recorded on the loan account. Any adjustment that might be resulted therefrom shall be reflected on the financial statements in the subsequent year.
observations.managementAction: There was already a reconciliation with the bank concern and said finding was addressed

observations.recommendation:
3.) All expenses incurred should be recognized as expenses in the period in which they incurred and reflected them in the books of accounts. Necessary adjustments for the effect of said transactions should be done.
4.) We recommended that the Office of the Accounting Office should prepare the Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statement, effect the necessary adjustment, if any, and submit the same to COA within 15 days pursuant to COA Circular No. 96-011 and 92-125A dated March 4, 1992.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive who is primarily responsible for all government funds and property:
1.) Directs the Municipal Engineer to submit accomplishment reports on the projects undertaken by LGU, and Municipal Treasurer to submit completely Disbursement Vouchers (DV) and supporting documents (SD) to the Municipal Accountant for proper booking up and/or recording the same in the books of accounts and submitted the same to the Commission.
2.) Make a necessary adjustments for failure to include the amount of FMR in the PPE as suggested below:
Road Networks 1,179,000.00
Government Equity 1,179,000.00

The management must make a bank reconciliation and submit the same to the auditor concerned. A reconciliation of the records must be made to correct the difference of the amount recorded. Any adjustment that might be resulted therefrom shall be reflected on the financial statements in the subsequent year.

The management of the LGU must secure clear understanding with the Source Agency as to the ownership of the PPE should the latter intended that the PPE is given to the LGU Shariff Aguak or the SA retained ownership of the PPE thereof but held in trust by the LGU. And submit the same to the auditor concerned.

We recommend that management shall advise all concerned officers to prepare all supporting documents pertaining to the said cash advances and apply for the relief of accountability or write-off to the Office of the Chairman, Commission on Audit, Quezon City.
observations.managementAction: This LGU is the epicenter of the political disputes between or among politicians and even reached the national attention. Upon inquiry, Mr. Danny Dimapalao was at-large or absconded sometimes in 1994. Per information, he died during the time of Late Governor Datu Andal Ampatuan, Sr. The latter was then the municipal mayor whom Mr. Dimapalao was the treasurer at the time. The situation was so volatile just because of the political condition of place. Martial Commission was declared affecting the officers of the said LGU. Thus changes in administration ensued.
observations.reason: Considering the condition of the place and dynamic changes of political officials including the support staff, thus this issue reach to this day.

observations.recommendation:
A reconciliation of the records between the LGU and DBP must be made to look
into the difference of the amount recorded on loan account. Any adjustment that
might be resulted therefrom shall be reflected on the financial statements in the
subsequent year.

3.) All expenses incurred should be recognized as expenses in the period in which
they incurred and reflected them in the books of accounts. Necessary
adjustments for the effect of said transactions should be done.
4.) We recommended that the Office of the Accounting Office should prepare the
Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statement, effect the necessary adjustment, if
any, and submit the same to COA within 15 days pursuant to COA Circular
No. 96-011 and 92-125A dated March 4, 1992.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive who is primarily responsible for
all government funds and property:
3.) directs the Municipal Engineer the accomplishment reports on the
projects undertaken by LGU, and Municipal Treasurer to submit
completely Disbursement Vouchers (DV) and supporting documents
(SD) to the Municipal Accountant for proper booking up and/or
recording the same in the books of accounts and submitted the same to
the Office of the Auditor within prescribed by the Commission.
4.) Make a necessary adjustments for failure to include the amount of
FMR in the PPE as suggested below:
Road Networks 1,179,000.00
Government Equity 1,179,000.00

The management must make a bank reconciliation and submit the same to the
auditor concerned. A reconciliation of the records must be made to correct the
difference of the amount recorded. Any adjustment that might be resulted
therefrom shall be reflected on the financial statements in the subsequent year.

The management of the LGU must secure clear understanding with the Source
Agency as to the ownership of the PPE should the latter intended that the PPE is
given to the LGU Shariff Aguak or the SA retained ownership of the PPE thereof
but held in trust by the LGU. And submit the same to the auditor concerned.

Recommendations
All funds received as trust for LGU’s implementation shall be recorded as TRUST
LIABILITY and not income and all expenses, including bank charges, related thereto
are capitalized as part of the cost of the project under trust. Necessary adjustments for the
effect of said transactions should be done.

Management’s Comment
The management had already noted and reviewed the COA Circular No. 94-013-Rules
and Regulations in the Grant, Utilization and Liquidation of Funds Transferred to
Implementing Agencies. The management is now strictly enforcing and ensuring that:
- The transfer is properly taken up in the local books.
- The transferred funds are used only for the intended purpose, and that
- Proper accounting and reporting is made of the utilization of the funds.

Furthermore, all funds received as trust for LGU’s implementation shall be now
recorded as TRUST LIABILITY and not income and all expenses related thereto are
capitalized as part of the cost of the project under trust. COA’s recommendation is
properly taken and noted.

Recommendation
1. The municipal accountant should submit the Report of Check Issued together with
disbursement voucher and the ORIGINAL supporting documents within the period
prescribed by Commission. Observe proper guidelines on disbursement in accordance
with PD 1445 and other existing rules and regulations
2. The municipal treasurer should not release the check for payment unless there was a
corresponding official receipt issued thereof by the payee;
3. The management i.e. concerned officials should notify the auditor within the
prescribed as recited above for the deliveries.
4. We recommend that the Local Chief Executive who is primarily responsible for all
governments funds and property, directs the Municipal Treasurer to submit
completely Disbursement Vouchers (DV) and supporting documents (SD) to the
Municipal Accountant for proper booking up and/or recording the same in the books
of accounts and submitted the same to the Office of the Auditor within prescribed
period by the Commission.
NOTA BENE:
SECTION 127. Administrative Disciplinary Action. – Subject to rules and
regulations as may be approved by the President (Prime Minister), any
unjustified failure by the public officer concerned to comply with any
requirement imposed in this Code shall constitute neglect of duty and shall be
a ground for administrative disciplinary action against the said public officer
who, upon being found guilty thereof after hearing, shall be meted out such
penalty as is commensurate with the degree of his guilt in accordance with the
Civil Service Law. Repeated unjustified failure to comply with the
requirements imposed in this Code shall be conclusive proof that the public
officer concerned is notoriously undesirable [PD 1445].

Recommendation
The municipal accountant should make an adjustment on the accounts stated and effect
the correction on the books of accounts.

ADJUSTMENT
Cash in Bank-LCCA 238,456.24
Loan Payable-Domestic - 238,456.24
238,456.24 238,456.24

Recommendations
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive who is primarily responsible for all
governments funds and property, directs the: Municipal Engineer to submit the
accomplishment on the various projects undertaken by the LGU; the Municipal Treasurer
to submit complete Disbursement Vouchers (DV) and supporting documents (SD) to the
Municipal Accountant for proper booking up and/or recording the same in the books of
accounts and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor within prescribed period by the
Commission.

The management must submit RCI and RD with all the originals of vouchers/payrolls, contracts, accomplishment reports and/or reports of completion and all supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor. Failure of the management to submit the same shall be a ground of any appropriate action against the responsible official(s).

1.The management must exclude/drop the Public Infrastructure Account i.e. Road Networks and Water Supply Systems totaling P26,715,211.53 as required under PPSAS and make necessary adjustments;
2.Reclassify Other Equipment to Other Machinery and Equipment amounting P38,000.00;

The management of the audited agency should submit the prepared GAD Plan and Budget to the Audit Team concerned. Continued failures of the management of the Audited Agency to comply with the above cited Circulars shall be a ground to an appropriate action against the officials concerned.

We recommend that management shall advise all concerned officers to prepare all supporting documents pertaining to the said cash advances and apply for the relief of accountability or write-off to the Office of the Chairman, Commission on Audit, Quezon City.
